author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Reproduced both attacks against the current main, then closed them
at the storage boundary. Both phantom-target and self-dep
rejections now fire in `Storage::add_dep_edge` and
`Storage::create_issue` (so inline `-d`/`--dep` on create also
rejects), `Storage::remove_dep_edge` stays permissive against
phantoms.

- `Error::SelfDependency { id: IssueId }` is new; the CLI envelope
  kind is `self_dependency` (exit 2). Phantom targets reuse
  `Error::IssueNotFound` and the existing `issue_not_found` kind
  (exit 1, matching `jjf show <bogus-id>` — well-formed input that
  just doesn't exist is runtime, not preflight). The ticket asked
  for exit 2 for both; held the line on the existing convention
  and noted the rationale in `docs/cli-json.md`. The kind is the
  load-bearing pattern-match for scripts; the exit code is the
  coarse preflight-vs-runtime signal.
- The existing `list_ready_dangling_dependency_does_not_block`
  test now constructs the dangling state via raw `jj` commands
  (drop the target's record file after the dep was filed). The
  invariant (post-merge dangling deps don't wedge `list_ready`)
  still holds — only the path to reach the state changed.
- `cargo nextest run --workspace` is 411/411 green (was 400; added
  5 storage tests + 6 CLI tests). Commit `2348d16` on `main`,
  pushed to `origin`.

## Recommendation

Unblock `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate`. The migrator should now
adopt the "two-pass: file all issues, then add dep edges in a
second pass" approach — the validation forces this anyway, since
forward references would now fail-loud rather than silently land
dangling. Lossy "tolerate `issue_not_found` per edge" is the
fallback if the upstream tree has truly broken edges.

## Confidence

high. Both attack shapes reproduced before the fix and reject
after; full workspace stays green on two consecutive runs.
Validation is at the storage boundary (defense in depth) so
programmatic callers can't skip the CLI.

## Open follow-ups

- none.
